name: Total bilirubin in blood
description: ## Definition & Clinical Context Bilirubin is a yellow-orange pigment produced when the body breaks down red blood cells, and **total bilirubin** is the sum of all its forms (conjugated + unconjugated) in a single blood sample [1]. It is a standard laboratory test — the source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser (most commonly on serum or plasma) or, especially in neonatal settings, by a blood-gas analyser via co-oximetry on whole blood [2]. It is collected in routine biochemistry, in intensive care (typically on venous samples, occasionally on arterial), and in neonatal units (often on capillary samples). Results are reported either in mass units (**mg/dL**) or in SI units (**µmol/L**). The reference measurement procedure for total bilirubin recognised by JCTLM (the Doumas method, listed by IFCC/BIPM/ILAC) reports results as amount-of-substance concentration in µmol/L [3], which is the metrological standard. Both units are in active use in practice; the choice varies by lab and by country. ## Included Concepts The set contains ten LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, grouped here by specimen type. Each specimen usually has two variants: a mass-concentration variant (mg/dL) and a substance-concentration variant (µmol/L). ### Serum or plasma (default specimen) Most common specimen for this test in hospital biochemistry; use these when the source does not specify a more precise specimen. - *3024128 / 1975-2 — Bilirubin.total [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: reported in mg/dL. - *3006140 / 14631-6 — Bilirubin.total [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: reported in µmol/L. ### Whole blood (unspecified) Used when the source reports total bilirubin on whole blood without specifying arterial, venous or capillary origin — typically a co-oximetry result from a blood-gas analyser when the specimen origin is not recorded. - *3028833 / 42719-5 — Bilirubin.total [Mass/volume] in Blood (LOINC)*: mg/dL. - *40757494 / 54363-7 — Bilirubin.total [Moles/volume] in Blood (LOINC)*: µmol/L. ### Arterial blood Used when the specimen is explicitly an arterial sample — most often an arterial blood-gas run on a point-of-care analyser that includes a co-oximetry bilirubin channel [2]. Less common than venous in most adult ICU workflows. - *40762888 / 59827-6 — Bilirubin.total [Mass/volume] in Arterial blood (LOINC)*: mg/dL. - *1175183 / 89872-6 — Bilirubin.total [Moles/volume] in Arterial blood (LOINC)*: µmol/L. ### Venous blood Used when the specimen is explicitly venous whole blood — the most common whole-blood specimen in adult ICU and ward settings, including venous blood-gas runs. - *40762889 / 59828-4 — Bilirubin.total [Mass/volume] in Venous blood (LOINC)*: mg/dL. - *1175191 / 89871-8 — Bilirubin.total [Moles/volume] in Venous blood (LOINC)*: µmol/L. ### Capillary blood Primarily used in neonatal screening (heel-prick) and point-of-care testing. Only the SI variant exists in LOINC. - *1616780 / 97770-2 — Bilirubin.total [Moles/volume] in Capillary blood (LOINC)*: µmol/L. ### Serum, plasma or blood (unspecified, generic) Generic concept when the specimen is not differentiated between serum/plasma and whole blood. Only the SI variant exists. - *46235782 / 77137-8 — Bilirubin.total [Moles/volume] in Serum, Plasma or Blood (LOINC)*: µmol/L. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows the LOINC property: all mass-concentration concepts use **mg/dL** (concept 8840), all substance-concentration concepts use **µmol/L** (concept 8749). Conversion: 1 mg/dL = 17.1 µmol/L; 1 µmol/L = 0.0585 mg/dL. ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded. The set is defined by anchoring on the two LOINC groups for total bilirubin (mass concentration and substance concentration) and pulling in all their descendants, which naturally excludes related but distinct bilirubin measurements: conjugated (direct) bilirubin, unconjugated (indirect) bilirubin, neonatal transcutaneous bilirubin, delta bilirubin, bilirubin.glucuronidated, and bilirubin in urine or amniotic fluid. These belong to separate concept sets. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "total bilirubin" without specifying a specimen but is known to come from a routine biochemistry analyser: `3024128 / 1975-2 — Bilirubin.total [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma` if the value is in mg/dL, `3006140 / 14631-6 — Bilirubin.total [Moles/volume] in Serum or Plasma` if the value is in µmol/L. Serum or plasma is by far the most common specimen for this test. - **Serum/plasma vs serum/plasma/blood**: use `46235782 / 77137-8 — Bilirubin.total [Moles/volume] in Serum, Plasma or Blood` only when the source genuinely cannot tell whether the matrix is serum/plasma or whole blood (e.g. a column aggregating results from both biochemistry and blood-gas analysers without a matrix tag). When the workflow is a routine biochemistry analyser, prefer `3024128 / 1975-2` (or `3006140 / 14631-6` in µmol/L), even if the matrix is not explicitly labelled in the source — `77137-8` is a generic fallback, not the default. - **Blood-gas / ICU context**: if the result comes from a venous blood-gas analyser, map to `40762889 / 59828-4 — Bilirubin.total [Mass/volume] in Venous blood` (mg/dL) or `1175191 / 89871-8 — Bilirubin.total [Moles/volume] in Venous blood` (µmol/L). For an arterial blood-gas analyser, use `40762888 / 59827-6 — Bilirubin.total [Mass/volume] in Arterial blood` or `1175183 / 89872-6 — Bilirubin.total [Moles/volume] in Arterial blood`. - **Capillary / neonatal heel-prick**: map to `1616780 / 97770-2 — Bilirubin.total [Moles/volume] in Capillary blood`. Since no mg/dL variant exists, convert mg/dL to µmol/L (×17.1) before mapping. - **Do not confuse with**: direct/conjugated bilirubin, indirect/unconjugated bilirubin, delta bilirubin, transcutaneous (skin, non-invasive) bilirubin, neonatal bilirubin panels, or bilirubin in urine — all belong to other concept sets. - **Method is not part of the mapping target**: the source lab's measurement method (diazo, spectrophotometric, dry chemistry, co-oximetry, etc.) can be preserved in the measurement source value but does not change which concept to map to. ## References [1] Kalakonda A, Jenkins BA, John S. Physiology, Bilirubin. In: StatPearls. Treasure Island (FL): StatPearls Publishing; 2023. Available: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK470290/ [2] Liu J, Dai Y, Li R, Yuan J, Wang Q, Wang L. Accuracy and Reliability of Whole Blood Bilirubin Measurements Using a Roche Blood Gas Analyzer for Neonatal Hyperbilirubinemia Screening and Risk Stratification. Front Pediatr. 2022;10:910566. Available: https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fped.2022.910566/full [3] Joint Committee for Traceability in Laboratory Medicine (JCTLM). Database of higher-order reference measurement procedures, reference materials and reference measurement services. BIPM/IFCC/ILAC. Available: https://www.jctlm.org/database/
